openmeter/streaming/clickhouse.purpose: Concrete streaming.Connector implementation against ClickHouse's single shared events table (namespace+type+subject+time ORDER BY key). Every query type is a distinct query-struct with a toSQL() method, keeping business logic isolated from the Connector and enabling SQL-assertion unit tests without a live ClickHouse.

patterns.name: Query-struct with toSQL()
patterns.description: Each query type (queryMeter, queryEventsTable, InsertEventsQuery, listSubjectsQuery, etc.) is a plain Go struct with a toSQL() or ToSQL() method returning (string, []interface{}). Connector methods are thin delegators that construct the struct and call toSQL(). Business logic lives in the struct, not in the Connector.
patterns.example: query := queryMeter{Database: c.config.Database, EventsTableName: c.config.EventsTableName, Namespace: namespace, Meter: meter, ...}; sql, args, err := query.toSQL()

patterns.name: sqlbuilder.ClickHouse for all SQL construction
patterns.description: All SQL is built via github.com/huandu/go-sqlbuilder with ClickHouse flavour. Never use fmt.Sprintf for WHERE clauses or value interpolation — use builder.Var() / builder.Equal() / builder.Where() to keep parameterisation safe and SQL assertions stable in tests.

patterns.name: columnFactory for table-qualified column references
patterns.description: Use columnFactory(eventsTableName) to get a func(string) string emitting table-qualified column names (e.g. om_events.namespace). Required whenever the query uses CTEs or joins that could introduce ambiguous column names.

patterns.name: toCountRowSQL companion for progress tracking
patterns.description: Every query struct that may be tracked via progressmanager must also implement toCountRowSQL() counting only by ORDER-BY columns (namespace, type, subject, time) for a cheap estimate. Connector checks params.ClientID != nil before calling withProgressContext.
patterns.example: if params.ClientID != nil { countSQL, countArgs := query.toCountRowSQL(); ctx, err = c.withProgressContext(ctx, namespace, *params.ClientID, countSQL, countArgs) }

patterns.name: ClickHouse error-code 60 mapped to domain errors
patterns.description: Map ClickHouse code 60 (table/view not found) to models.NewNamespaceNotFoundError or meter.NewMeterNotFoundError by string-matching err.Error() for 'code: 60'. Never let the raw ClickHouse error escape to callers.

patterns.name: selectCustomerIdColumn + customersWhere for customer filtering
patterns.description: Customer filtering requires two coordinated calls: selectCustomerIdColumn builds a WITH map CTE and adds customer_id to SELECT; customersWhere adds the IN (subjects) WHERE clause. Both accept the eventsTableName for table-qualified references.
patterns.example: query = selectCustomerIdColumn(d.EventsTableName, *d.Customers, query); query = customersWhere(d.EventsTableName, *d.Customers, query)

patterns.name: SkipCreateTables=true in unit tests
patterns.description: New() calls createTable on startup; mock ClickHouse connections have no Exec setup for DDL. Always set Config.SkipCreateTables = true when constructing a Connector against a mock.
patterns.example: config := Config{..., SkipCreateTables: true}; connector, err := New(context.Background(), config)

key_file_guides.role: Public surface: implements streaming.Connector, owns Config validation, table creation on startup, and orchestrates all query structs. Method bodies are thin delegators.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config.SkipCreateTables must be true in unit tests; otherwise New() calls createTable on a mock with no Exec setup and will panic or error.

key_file_guides.role: Most complex query struct. Handles all aggregation types (sum/avg/min/max/count/uniqExact/argMax), window-size tumble functions, group-by JSON extraction, stored-at filter, and PREWHERE split. Also owns NullDecimal scanner and scanRows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Month window size requires explicit toDateTime(..., tz) cast to avoid Date-vs-DateTime scan mismatch. The from() method merges query.From with Meter.EventFrom, always taking the later of the two.

key_file_guides.role: Handles v1 event listing (queryEventsTable), count (queryCountEvents), and batch insert (InsertEventsQuery). Table DDL (createEventsTable) lives here too.
key_file_guides.watch_for: InsertEventsQuery.ToSQL is exported (capital T) unlike all other query structs. The column order in INSERT must match the table definition exactly.

key_file_guides.role: Cursor-paginated v2 event listing using store_row_id as tie-breaker. Cursor conditions differ between ASC and DESC sort orders.
key_file_guides.watch_for: customersWhere in v2 passes the full tableName (database.table) while v1 passes only eventsTableName — keep consistent with the helper's expectation.

key_file_guides.role: Shared helpers: selectCustomerIdColumn (WITH map CTE), customersWhere, subjectWhere, columnFactory.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetUsageAttribution().GetValues() may return customer key + subject keys; both are mapped in the WITH clause so the customer_id lookup works for either identifier.

key_file_guides.role: Provides CHTestSuite: skips integration tests unless TEST_CLICKHOUSE_DSN is set, creates a unique temp database per test, and drops it on success only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use t.Context() not context.Background() in tests; CreateTempDatabase cleanup intentionally skips DROP on test failure to aid debugging.

key_file_guides.role: MockClickHouse and MockRows for unit tests that don't need a real ClickHouse connection.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MockClickHouse.Query must be set up before calls to queryMeter/queryEventsTable methods since the Connector calls Query synchronously.

anti_patterns: Adding raw fmt.Sprintf string interpolation for query values — always use sqlbuilder.Var() or builder parameters to prevent injection and keep test SQL assertions stable.
Calling c.config.ClickHouse.Query/Exec directly in a new Connector method without going through a query-struct toSQL() — loses the testable SQL-assertion pattern.
Returning raw ClickHouse errors to callers — map code 60 to domain errors (NamespaceNotFoundError, MeterNotFoundError) before returning.
Adding window-size logic to scanRows — scanRows only scans; toSQL handles all window column selection.
Skipping toCountRowSQL on a new query struct that supports progress tracking via ClientID — withProgressContext must be called before executing the main query.

decisions.decision: Single shared events table across all namespaces with namespace as the leading ORDER BY column
decisions.rationale: Avoids per-namespace table creation/deletion overhead; ClickHouse partition pruning by toYYYYMM(time) and the (namespace, type, subject, toStartOfHour(time)) sort key give adequate read performance for typical multi-tenant query patterns.

decisions.decision: Query structs with toSQL() instead of inline SQL in Connector methods
decisions.rationale: Enables unit tests to assert exact SQL output without a real ClickHouse connection, and keeps each query's logic isolated and independently testable.

decisions.decision: store_row_id (ULID) as cursor tie-breaker in v2 pagination
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse DateTime precision is seconds; multiple events in the same second would be non-deterministically ordered without a unique tie-breaker. ULIDs are time-ordered and unique, so store_row_id > cursor.ID correctly pages through same-second events.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new meter aggregation query that must be unit-testable

openmeter/streaming/retry.purpose: Decorator that wraps any streaming.Connector with automatic retry logic for transient ClickHouse errors (EOF, connection-acquire timeout, cluster-reshard error code). BatchInsert and ValidateJSONPath are intentionally not retried to preserve idempotency.

patterns.name: Decorator / pass-through wrapper
patterns.description: Connector embeds no state beyond the downstreamConnector and retry config. Every streaming.Connector method either calls withRetry wrapping the downstream call, or delegates directly (BatchInsert, ValidateJSONPath, CreateNamespace, DeleteNamespace do not retry).
patterns.example: func (c *Connector) QueryMeter(...) ([]meter.MeterQueryRow, error) { return withRetry(ctx, c, func() ([]meter.MeterQueryRow, error) { return c.downstreamConnector.QueryMeter(ctx, namespace, m, params) }) }

patterns.name: Config.Validate() enforced by New()
patterns.description: New() calls config.Validate() and returns an error if validation fails. Tests that want to bypass config validation for withRetry isolation should construct the Connector struct directly.
patterns.example: c, err := New(Config{DownstreamConnector: ..., Logger: slog.Default(), RetryWaitDuration: 100*time.Millisecond, MaxTries: 3})

patterns.name: Selective retry via RetryIf predicate
patterns.description: Only io.ErrUnexpectedEOF, io.EOF, clickhouse.ErrAcquireConnTimeout, and chproto.ErrAllConnectionTriesFailed are retryable. All other errors fail immediately. Add new retryable errors to the RetryIf closure in withRetry.
patterns.example: retry.RetryIf(func(err error) bool { if errors.Is(err, io.ErrUnexpectedEOF) { return true }; ...; return false })

patterns.name: CombineDelay(BackOffDelay, RandomDelay) with configurable MaxDelay
patterns.description: avast/retry-go is used with backoff + random jitter to avoid thundering-herd reconnect storms. MaxDelay caps exponential growth; set it to a non-zero value to prevent unbounded delays.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: Config struct, Connector struct, New constructor, per-method wrappers, and the generic withRetry[T any] helper using avast/retry-go with backoff+jitter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BatchInsert is intentionally NOT wrapped in withRetry — retrying inserts could cause duplicate event ingestion. CreateNamespace/DeleteNamespace and ValidateJSONPath also bypass retry intentionally.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for Config validation, retry behaviour, context cancellation stopping retries, and backoff producing increasing delays.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests construct Connector directly (not via New()) to skip config validation when testing withRetry in isolation — this is intentional, not a bug.

anti_patterns: Wrapping BatchInsert with withRetry — duplicate event inserts on retry break idempotency guarantees.
Adding application-level errors (e.g. MeterNotFoundError) to the RetryIf predicate — only transient infrastructure errors should be retried.
Setting MaxDelay to 0 — Config.Validate() accepts 0 as valid (non-negative) but retry-go interprets 0 as no cap, potentially causing very long delays under exponential backoff.
Constructing the Connector without calling New() in production code — always use New() to ensure config validation runs.

decisions.decision: Backoff + random jitter (CombineDelay) with configurable MaxDelay
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse cluster restarts during rolling updates cause transient connection errors; backoff with jitter avoids thundering-herd reconnect storms from multiple concurrent workers.

Retrying inserts on transient errors would produce duplicate rows in the events table, breaking the exactly-once ingestion guarantee. The sink worker's three-phase flush ordering handles retry at a higher level.

code_examples.scenario: Wrap a new streaming.Connector method with retry (read-only operations only)

openmeter/streaming/testutils.purpose: In-memory MockStreamingConnector for tests that need a streaming.Connector without a real ClickHouse. Supports AddSimpleEvent/SetSimpleEvents with optional WithStoredAt, AddRow for pre-canned exact results, and approximate aggregation that mirrors ClickHouse tumble-window semantics.

patterns.name: AddSimpleEvent for usage-based test setup
patterns.description: Register per-meter events with AddSimpleEvent(meterSlug, value, time, opts...). Use WithStoredAt(t) to set a StoredAt different from the event Time, exercising stored-at cutoff logic in billing finalisation. Events are auto-sorted by Time ASC after each add.

patterns.name: AddRow for exact pre-canned QueryMeter results
patterns.description: When the approximate aggregation is insufficient, use AddRow(meterSlug, meter.MeterQueryRow{...}) to register exact results. QueryMeter returns rows whose WindowStart/WindowEnd match params.From/To exactly. AddRow takes precedence over SimpleEvents for that slug.
patterns.example: conn.AddRow("meter-key", meter.MeterQueryRow{WindowStart: from, WindowEnd: to, Value: 42.0, GroupBy: map[string]*string{}})

patterns.name: Reset() between subtests
patterns.description: Call conn.Reset() to clear all rows and events between test cases that share a connector instance. AddSimpleEvent appends; rows accumulate across calls unless Reset() is called.

patterns.name: filterStoredAt uses Unix-second precision
patterns.description: The mock's filterStoredAt evaluates FilterTimeUnix using Unix() seconds, matching ClickHouse's DateTime column precision. Tests relying on sub-second StoredAt distinctions will not work correctly.
patterns.example: // Use full-second boundaries for StoredAt filters in tests; sub-second differences are ignored

patterns.name: aggregateEvents returns MeterNotFoundError for unregistered slugs
patterns.description: If a meterSlug has never been registered via AddSimpleEvent or AddRow, QueryMeter returns meter.NewMeterNotFoundError. Always call AddSimpleEvent at least once, or use SetSimpleEvents(slug, func(e []SimpleEvent) []SimpleEvent { return e }) to register an empty slice.
patterns.example: conn.SetSimpleEvents("my-meter", func(e []SimpleEvent) []SimpleEvent { return e }) // register without events

key_file_guides.role: Entire package implementation: MockStreamingConnector, SimpleEvent, AddSimpleEvent, SetSimpleEvents, AddRow, QueryMeter aggregation logic, filterStoredAt helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: aggregateEvents returns MeterNotFoundError if the meterSlug has never been registered — always register the meter slug before calling QueryMeter.

key_file_guides.role: Comprehensive test cases for the mock's approximation accuracy: windowed vs non-windowed, LATEST aggregation, StoredAt filtering, empty-window row suppression.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests import openmeter/testutils (not this package) for GetRFC3339Time helpers — be careful about import cycles when adding new test helpers.

anti_patterns: Using MockStreamingConnector for tests that need real ClickHouse SQL behaviour (PREWHERE, decimal precision edge cases) — use ConnectorTestSuite with TEST_CLICKHOUSE_DSN instead.
Registering events for a meter slug after calling QueryMeter on that slug within the same test without Reset() — AddSimpleEvent appends; rows accumulate across calls.
Relying on sub-second StoredAt precision in FilterTimeUnix predicates — the mock truncates to Unix seconds matching ClickHouse DateTime behaviour.
Using MockStreamingConnector without passing a testing.TB to NewMockStreamingConnector — always pass t for proper test helper registration.

decisions.decision: Approximate aggregation rather than full SQL semantics
decisions.rationale: A full SQL emulation would be complex and fragile. The approximation is accurate enough for service-layer tests (billing charges, entitlement balance) while keeping the mock simple. Tests that need exact SQL behaviour use real ClickHouse via CHTestSuite.

decisions.decision: AddRow takes precedence over SimpleEvents for the same slug
decisions.rationale: Allows tests to provide exact pre-canned results for complex scenarios (group-by, multi-window) without implementing full aggregation logic in the mock.

code_examples.scenario: Test a service that queries meter usage with a stored-at cutoff
